paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

review.summary: This ApJS paper presents a catalog of 591 high-velocity star candidates selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2. The LaTeX source contains nine tables (mostly deluxetable* inside longrotatetable environments), four of which are in the main text (tab:basic, tab:kinematic, tab:punbound_50, tab:origin, tab:strict_criteria) and four in the appendix (tab:vesc, tab:unbound, tab:atmospheric, tab:general_catalog). The primary data asset is an external CSV catalog hosted at http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv containing 93 columns for all 591 candidates. Additionally, the paper references the Open Fast Stars Catalog at https://faststars.space/ and survey/tool URLs (LAMOST DR5/DR7, TOPCAT). The appendix also includes a longtable (tab:general_catalog) documenting all 93 columns of the external catalog.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:basic — Basic parameters (R.A., decl., S/N, spectral type, RV, proper motions, parallax, Gaia magnitudes, astrometric flag) for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs.

source_refs.caption: Basic parameters for the 20 fastest High velocity star candidates (HiVelSCs)
source_refs.label: tab:basic

columns.name: S/N_r
columns.meaning: r-band spectral signal-to-noise ratio from LAMOST

columns.name: RV_LASP
columns.meaning: Radial velocity from LASP, zero-point corrected (km/s)

columns.name: pmra
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension from Gaia DR2 (mas/yr)

columns.name: pmdec
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination from Gaia DR2 (mas/yr)

columns.name: astrometric_flag
columns.meaning: Flag (=1) if astrometric parameters meet conservative quality criteria

internal_tables.evidence: Table in Section 2.4 lists observed and derived basic parameters for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs, with footnotes explaining column provenance.
internal_tables.comments: Only the 20 fastest candidates shown in print; full 591-row catalog is external (CSV).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:kinematic — Galactocentric spatial positions (x, y, z), distance, velocities (Vx, Vy, Vz, V_GC), and orbit parameters (e, Zmax, rmin, E-Phi) for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs.

source_refs.caption: Spatial Positions and Velocities of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs
source_refs.label: tab:kinematic

columns.name: Zmax
columns.meaning: Orbital maximum height above Galactic disk (kpc)

columns.name: rmin
columns.meaning: Minimum crossing radius during orbital trace-back (kpc)

columns.name: E-Phi(inf)
columns.meaning: Energy difference between orbital energy and potential at infinity (kpc^2/Myr^2)

internal_tables.evidence: Table in Section 2.4 presents derived kinematic and orbital parameters from Monte Carlo sampling of astrometry and seven Galactic potential models.
internal_tables.comments: Only 20 fastest shown; bound/unbound status and orbit parameters for full sample in external CSV.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:punbound_50 — Number of HiVelSCs with unbound probability >50% under each of seven Galactic potential models.

source_refs.caption: The number of HiVelSCs with probabilities of being unbound larger than 50% in each of the seven potential models
source_refs.label: tab:punbound_50

columns.name: Model
columns.meaning: Galactic potential model (Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008)

columns.name: N(Pub>=50%)
columns.meaning: Number of HiVelSCs with unbound probability ≥50%

internal_tables.evidence: Table in Section 2.4 summarizes unbound candidate counts across seven potential models, ranging from 43 (Watkins+2019) to 304 (Xue+2008).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:origin — Classification criteria dividing HiVelSCs into HVS, HRS, RS, and OUT types based on Pgc, PMW, and Pub thresholds.

columns.name: Class
columns.meaning: Origin class: HVS, HRS, RS, or OUT

columns.name: Pgc
columns.meaning: Probability of ejection from the Galactic Center threshold

columns.name: PMW
columns.meaning: Probability of ejection from the Galactic disk threshold

internal_tables.evidence: Table in Section 4 defines the criteria used to classify 591 HiVelSCs into four origin types.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:strict_criteria — Results of applying progressively more conservative selection criteria: number of HiVelSCs remaining and unbound counts.

source_refs.caption: The results using more stringent selection criteria
source_refs.label: tab:strict_criteria

columns.name: N1
columns.meaning: Total number of HiVelSCs satisfying criteria

columns.name: N2
columns.meaning: Number unbound in Watkins+2019 with Pub≥50%

columns.name: N3
columns.meaning: Number unbound in Xue+2008 with Pub≥50%

columns.name: N4
columns.meaning: Number bound in Xue+2008 with Pub<50%

columns.name: Maximum V_GC
columns.meaning: Maximum Galactocentric velocity among selected HiVelSCs (km/s)

internal_tables.evidence: Table in Section 5.2 shows how stricter astrometric and velocity-precision cuts reduce the sample from 591 to 92 conservative candidates.
internal_tables.comments: Five-row summary table for sensitivity analysis.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:vesc (Appendix) — Escape velocities at each star's position under seven Galactic potential models, for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs.

source_refs.caption: Escape velocities of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs in the seven Galactic potential models
source_refs.label: tab:vesc

internal_tables.evidence: Appendix Table A1 lists escape velocities computed for the 20 fastest candidates across all seven potential models used in the study.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:unbound (Appendix) — Unbound probabilities Pgc, PMW, and Pub under seven potential models for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs, plus origin classification.

source_refs.caption: Unbound probabilities of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs in the seven potential models
source_refs.label: tab:unbound

columns.name: Pgc
columns.meaning: Probability of ejection from the Galactic Center

columns.name: PMW
columns.meaning: Probability of ejection from the Galactic disk

columns.name: Pub(W)–Pub(X)
columns.meaning: Unbound probability under each of seven potential models

internal_tables.evidence: Appendix Table A2 presents ejection and unbound probabilities for the 20 fastest candidates, with origin classification in the last column.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:atmospheric (Appendix) — Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) from LASP and DD-Payne, plus [α/Fe] from DD-Payne, for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs.

source_refs.caption: Atmospheric parameters for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs
source_refs.label: tab:atmospheric

internal_tables.evidence: Appendix Table A3 shows atmospheric parameters from two independent pipelines (LASP and DD-Payne) for the 20 fastest candidates.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Tab:general_catalog (Appendix) — Column-by-column description of the full 93-column HiVelSC catalog, specifying name, units, and description for each field.

columns.name: Description
columns.meaning: Explanation of the column content and data provenance

internal_tables.evidence: Appendix Table A4 is a 93-row data dictionary documenting every column of the LAMOSTDR7-GAIADR2-HiVelSC catalog, with superscripts indicating LAMOST, Gaia, or derived provenance.
internal_tables.comments: Data dictionary for the external CSV catalog.

external_resources.description: Main data catalog: CSV file containing 93 columns for all 591 high-velocity star candidates, including LAMOST radial velocities and atmospheric parameters, Gaia DR2 astrometry and photometry, derived Galactocentric positions/velocities, escape velocities under seven potential models, unbound/ejection probabilities, and origin classification.

source_refs.context: A catalog for these 591 HiVelSCs, including radial velocities, atmospheric parameters, Gaia astrometric parameters, spatial positions, and velocities, etc., is  available at \url{http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv}.

external_resources.evidence: Explicitly provided in the abstract as the primary data product URL; the appendix Table A4 (tab:general_catalog) documents all 93 columns of this catalog.

external_resources.description: Open Fast Stars Catalog (OFSC), a community collection of 558 high-velocity star candidates from the literature, used by the authors for cross-matching and comparison with their new 591-candidate sample.

source_refs.context: only 14 of them were reported in the current version of the open fast stars catalog (OFSC) \footnote{https://faststars.space/}

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.4 citing the OFSC URL; the OFSC is used as a comparison sample throughout the paper.

external_resources.description: LAMOST Data Release 7 access page — the spectroscopic survey providing radial velocities and atmospheric parameters for the 591 HiVelSCs.

source_refs.context: DR7 of Low-Resolution Spectroscopic Survey provided 10,608,416 low resolution spectra \footnote{http://dr7.lamost.org/}

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.1 referencing the LAMOST DR7 data release URL.
external_resources.comments: Survey archive URL; the underlying LAMOST spectra are publicly available here but are not the paper's direct data product.

external_resources.description: TOPCAT tool used for cross-matching LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 within a 5-arcsec radius to produce the LAMOST-Gaia input catalog.

source_refs.context: Using the TOPCAT \footnote{http://www.star.bris.ac.uk/~mbt/topcat/}, cross-match of LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 were performed with a radius of 5 arc sec

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.3 describing the cross-match tool.
external_resources.comments: Software tool URL, not a data product.
